description: This collection comprises short stories, poems, a little bit of history and a little bit of ornithology, all with ruby connections. The reader greatly enjoyed searching out suitable gems for inclusion in this celebration of her own Ruby Wedding Anniversary.

title: O. Henry Memorial Award Prize Stories of 1921
description: In 1918 the Society of Arts and Sciences established the O. Henry Memorial Awards to reward the very best short stories published during the previous year with cash prizes. 1921 was a particularly productive year with sterling and brilliantly varied examples of the art of short story writing as are included here. The selection committee struggled to agree on these, but finally, here they are for us to enjoy and record. The length varies but most are sufficiently long for a reader to get their teeth into and enjoy. The stories are listed in a rough order of how they were judged, but all are excellent. Note that one story, 'Martin Gerrity Gets Even' actually had two authors, Courtney Ryley Cooper, and Leo F. Creagan but only one is able to be credited - Summary by Phil Chenevert

title: Fireside Christmas Short Stories
description: A collection of Christmas-themed short stories, intended to warm the heart and share with the family. Each story or poem in this collection is unique: some make us pause to consider the meaning of Christmas, others entertain and make us smile. So curl up in front of a blazing fire and be transported back to Christmases past. (Summary by Lynne Thompson)

title: Times' Red Cross Story Book By Famous Novelists Serving In His Majesty's Forces
description: These eighteen stories were published in 1915, in the midst of World War I. "Published For The Times' Fund For The Sick And Wounded" - Summary by Book's title page and david wales

title: Local Color Collection Vol. 001
description: In this celebration of diversity, learn about the myriad histories and cultures behind our volunteers. (summary by Eric Ray)

title: Stories in Black and White
description: This is a collection of short mystery stories, written in very different styles by eight different authors. - Summary by Carolin

title: English Communication Hub
author: Feen barry
description:
Welcome to English Communication Hub—your go-to podcast for improving your English speaking and communication skills with confidence.
Whether you're a beginner or an advanced learner, each episode is packed with practical English conversations, everyday vocabulary, pronunciation tips, speaking strategies, and communication techniques to help you sound more natural and fluent.
Learn how to speak confidently in real-life situations, job interviews, presentations, business meetings, and daily conversations. Our goal is to make English learning simple, engaging, and useful for your personal, academic, and professional success.

title: VetLaunch Lab: Companion Animal Commercial Intelligence
author: VetLaunch Lab
description: VetLaunch Lab decodes the business side of companion animal health — regulatory moves, product launches, pricing shifts, and pipeline bets — and translates them into what they actually mean for brand and commercial teams. Each episode breaks down the week's signals from across the industry's major players, cutting through the noise to answer the one question that matters: so what does this mean for your strategy? Built for marketing, commercial, and brand leaders at animal health companies who need to stay ahead of the competition, not just informed about it.

title: Short Story Classics (American) Volume One
description: A 1905 collection of ten short stories by American authors, including Washington Irving and Edgar Allan Poe. (Summary by Verla Viera)

title: What the Tech? Season 2
author: ProServeIT
description: Immerse yourself in our What The Tech podcast to discover the limitless possibilities of technology and its transformative impact on your business. Gain valuable insights on how Microsoft technology can propel your company to new heights. Subscribe now and join the forefront of the digital age.

title: Love Stories Volume 3
description: Here are 20 more love stories under an hour each, covering all kinds of experiences involving romantic or other kinds of love resulting in heartbreak or happiness -- where the heart overflows with passion, pride, emotion or wonder. ( ~ Michele Fry)

title: Best British Short Stories of 1922
description: Twenty-four short stories by famous and not-so-famous British authors. (Summary by David Wales) Note: A shortened section 21 was replaced on Dec 30, 2014 with the full text.

title: Doctor's Red Lamp
description: A fictional series of short stories from various authors, each containing stories of doctors. The compiled set of stories has various ranges, from stories of a sad and tragic ending, to those that have a romantic finish. Cast for Section 27. A Gentle Maniac, by George Edgar Montgomery: Stage Directions: Elijah Fisher Mr. Valdingam: J S Whitman Henry Van Hyde M. D: Beeswaxcandle Susan Valdingam: Sonia Rose: valroth Richard, a servant: Brian Fullen - Summary by ej400

title: Best Short Stories of 1917
description: I deny that the American short story is at a low ebb, and I offer the present volume as a revelation of the best that is now being done in this field. I agree with Mrs. Colum that the best stories are only to be found after a laborious dusty search, but this is the proof rather than the refutation of my position.[excerpt from Introduction] - Summary by Edward J. O'Brien, Editor

title: Yiddish Tales (יידיש מעשה)
description: A collection of 48 wonderful English language stories from Sholem Alechem, I. L. Perez, Shalom Asch, and others. Tales of humour and drama, tragedy and pathos set mostly in the Jewish communities of 19th-century eastern Europe, Russia, and the Ukraine. Translated from Yiddish by Helena Frank. (Summary by Adrian Praetzellis) Proof-listening by Ans Wink, K. Fowler, & Aron Horovitz

title: From a Swedish Homestead
description: ”From a Swedish Homestead” by the Swedish author Selma Lagerloef (translated by Jessie Brochner) is a varied collection of stories, mostly set in Dalarne or Vaermland in Sweden, but also some stories or legends from Kungahalla on the west-coast at the time between Heathendom and early Christianity plus some Legends from Italy and Belgium. The first nine sections, “The Story of a Country House”, is a short Novel, originally published on its own, but here part of the collection. It is the story of how a young orphan girl, Ingrid, is the instrument of saving the student Gunnar Hede, who has lost his wits and memory by a terrible experience, trying to save his home, his Country House, and who wanders the roads as a Pedlar, but at the same time afraid of most everything, especially animals. As told by Lagerloef it is a both fascinating and sweet story, as are all the others. (Summary by Lars Rolander)

title: Triumphs of Eugene Valmont
description: Short stories by a colleague of Jerome K. Jerome, and friend of Sir Arthur Conan Doyle. Barr probably wrote the first parody of Sherlock Holmes (included in this collection). He co-edited "The Idler" with Jerome. (written by Czechchris)

title: Dreams Collection 3 - Stories and Poems
description: This is a collection of 20 stories and/or poems, contributed by LibriVox volunteers, pertaining to dreams. - Summary by Michele Fry

title: American Women's Literature, 1847 to 1922
description: This is a collection of 20 short stories and long-form poetry by American women writers. (Summary by BellonaTimes)

title: Invisible Links
description: Selma Lagerlöf was born in Vaermland, Sweden, in 1858 and enjoyed a long and very successful career as a writer, receiving the Nobel-Price in Literature in 1909. She died in Vaermland in 1940. Invisible Links (Osynliga länkar) is a collection of short stories with an underlying theme about the links that influence and guide people’s actions and lives. It was first published in 1894 and the English translation in 1895. The stories are often set in Lagerloef’s Vaermland, but they also depict legends and history of Sweden, and some have connections to other works by Lagerloef. Invisible Links is a good introduction to the writings of Selma Lagerloef. (Summary by Lars Rolander)

title: Movies and Hollywood Short Story Collection, Volume 1
description: Fiction about (or involving) motion pictures started appearing in the late nineteenth-century, when writers first became aware of early kinetoscope technologies. These stories grew more and more popular as the public became increasingly fascinated with the movies, the film industry, and the odd inhabitants of Hollywood. These stories reflect and often respond to the public's fascination with the movies; at the same time, they also reveal their fears and anxieties about the new medium. The first volume of this anthology collects 16 short stories and a monologue about motion picture technology and the film industry published between 1895 and 1922. (Summary by ChuckW)

title: Lock and Key Library: Old Time English
description: LOCK AND KEY LIBRARY OLD TIME ENGLISH A collection of classic mystery and detective stories, collected and edited by Julian Hawthorne.Note: the second chapter of Dickens’ The Haunted House is not included in this edition. (Summary by Julie VW)

title: Rivals of Sherlock Holmes, Vol. 1
description: The Rivals of Sherlock Holmes was a British TV series from the early 70s that dramatized stories written contemporaneously with the Sherlock Holmes stories of Sir Arthur Conan Doyle. This anthology contains twelve of the thirteen stories adapted as episodes for the first season. Sadly, one story, "The Missing Witness Sensation" by Ernest Bramah, is not public domain in the U.S., but can be read in the anthology Max Carrados Mysteries. (Summary by Nullifidian)

title: Christmas Short Works Collection 2019
description: 2019 collection of items with a Christmas theme containing traditional stories, Christmas traditions, Christmas cakes. We hope you will enjoy it.

title: A to Zed Collection Vol. 001
description: A collection of pieces, both fiction and non-fiction, that have as its subject a word beginning with a specific letter of the English alphabet. Subjects can range from coffee to tea, animals to vampires, law to emotions.

title: Budget of Christmas Tales
description: This is a 1895 collection of christmas-themed short stories and poems by various authors, Charles Dickens himself being the most prominent feature. - Summary by Carolin

title: Hearth and Home: Collected Poems and Stories
description: Hearth and Home was a monthly story paper that ran from 1884 to 1933. It consisted of serials, short stories, poems, Q&A and correspondence, and articles on embroidery and lace-making. Unfortunately, of the 577 Hearth and Home issues, only 4 surviving issues are available online. The following are the short stories and poems found in those 4 issues. - Summary by Elsie Selwyn

title: Kaffir, Kangaroo, Klondike; Tales Of The Gold Fields
description: Seven short stories. - Summary by david wales

title: Enlighten Up With Christy
author: Christy Dreiling
description: Join Christy on Enlighten Up, a heart-centered podcast dedicated to exploring the many facets of love, self-love, relationships, purpose, and spirituality. Dive into enriching conversations and inspiring stories that will uplift your spirit and illuminate your path. Whether you're seeking guidance on nurturing your relationships, discovering your true purpose, or deepening your spiritual practice, Christy brings wisdom, compassion, and a touch of humor to each episode. Tune in and transform your journey with insightful interviews, practical tips, and heartfelt love

title: Wit and Humor of America, Vol 06
description: The Wit and Humor of America is a 10 volume series. In this, the sixth volume, 55 short stories and poems have been gathered from 42 authors. This volume is sure to delight listeners. (Summary by Linette Geisel)

title: Imaginotions - Truthless Tales
description: This is a collection of short stories by Tudor Jenks. Those stories are all written and composed in the way of a fairy tale, but they are not primarily aimed at children. The stories contain excellent satire, and are generally very funny and enjoyable. - Summary by Carolin

title: Little Masterpieces of American Wit and Humor Vol 2
description: Volume 2 of a ten volume collection of amusing tales, observations and anecdotes by America's greatest wordsmiths. This work includes selections by such household favorites as Ambrose Bierce, Oliver Wendell Holmes, Mark Twain and Bret Harte. - Summary by Lynne Thompson

title: Group of Noble Dames
description: The pedigrees of our county families, arranged in diagrams on the pages of county histories, mostly appear at first sight to be as barren of any touch of nature as a table of logarithms. But given a clue—the faintest tradition of what went on behind the scenes, and this dryness as of dust may be transformed into a palpitating drama. Out of such pedigrees and supplementary material most of the following stories have arisen and taken shape. (Summary adapted from the preface)

title: Changed Man And Other Tales
description: Eleven short stories. - Summary by david wales

title: Life's Little Ironies
description: Eighteen short stories by a master story teller. - Summary by david wales

title: Wessex Tales
description: Wessex Tales is a collection of six short stories written by Hardy in the 1880’s. If you’ve never read Hardy they’ll serve as a good introduction to his writing. Though not as comprehensive as his major works they do contain all the ingredients that make him instantly recognisable. (Introduction by T. Hynes.)

title: Ghost Ship & Other Stories
description: Richard Middleton is one of the many authors who, despite great merit, have been almost entirely forgotten today. This English author was an eminent literary figure in his day, admired by, among others, Edgar Jepson, Arthur Machen, and Raymond Chandler. This collection of short stories contains his best-known short story, The Ghost Ship, a skillfully written horror story. The other stories contained in this collection are not all horror stories, but cover a variety of genres and topics. - Summary by Carolin

title: Free, and other stories
description: Theodore Dreiser's first collection of short fiction, published in 1918. Dreiser was known primarily as a novelist (Sister Carrie, An American Tragedy, and others), but he was adept at the short story genre as well, having been a magazine editor at different times in his career. These stories illustrate Dreiser's bleak naturalistic philosophy of life. They often feature characters who have been marginalized by society and feel they are at the mercy of an indifferent universe that offers them few choices for a satisfying life. - Summary by James Hutchisson

title: Speak Turkish Like Crazy
author: Speak Turkish Like Crazy
description: Speak Turkish like crazy in no time! Improve your grammar and vocabulary with conversations, common phrases, and short stories even in your car.

title: Mr. Weston's Good Wine
description: This 1927 novel describes an evening in 1923 when Mr. Weston, who is apparently a wine merchant, but is evidently God, visits the fictional village of Folly Down in Dorset, and meets some of its individuals, whose backgrounds and lives leading up to this day are described during the course of the novel. Mr. Weston's colleague is named Michael, which is an allusion to the Archangel. For a while time stands still, and these individuals, according to their possessing qualities of good or evil, find their ultimate reward. - Summary by Wikipedia and David Wales

title: Lifted Masks
description: In this collection of short stories, Susan Glaspell examines the unique character of America and its people. (Summary by wildemoose)

title: Blazed Trail Stories and Stories Of The Wild Life
description: Thirteen short stories by a popular writer of the early 20th century (not to be confused with an earlier book Blazed Trail). White's books were popular at a time when America was losing its vanishing wilderness. He was a keen observer of the beauties of nature and human nature, yet could render them in a plain-spoken style. Based on his own experience, whether writing camping journals or Westerns, he included pithy and fun details about cabin-building, canoeing, logging, gold-hunting, and guns and fishing and hunting. He also interviewed people who had been involved in the fur trade, the California gold rush and other pioneers which provided him with details that give his novels verisimilitude. He salted in humor and sympathy for colorful characters such as canny Indian guides and "greenhorn" campers who carried too much gear. - Summary by Wikipedia and david wales

title: Soaked In Seaweed and 7 other nonsense novels
description: 8 great spoofs of 'types' of fiction by the premier Canadian humorist Leacock, taken from his book Nonsense Novels. The title of each parody gives away its genre: Soaked in Seaweed or, Upset in the Ocean; Maddened by Mystery: or, The Defective Detective; "Q." A Psychic Pstory of the Psupernatural; Guido the Gimlet of Ghent: A Romance of Chivalry; The Man in Asbestos: an Allegory of the Future; Sorrows of a Super Soul: or, The Memoirs of Marie Mushenough; A Hero in Homespun: or, The Life Struggle of Hezekiah Hayloft and Caroline's Christmas: or, The Inexplicable Infant. If you enjoy take offs and parodies, the stories in this collection are for you. See how many types you recognize as you listen and laugh. (Summary by phil chenevert)

title: Nonsense Novels
description: Ten silly stories by Canadian humourist Stephen Leacock. (Summary by Tricia G)

title: Frenzied Fiction
description: From the cave man to Santa Claus; spies, know-it-alls, and journalists: all are fair game for Leacock’s special brand of humor. He touches on the changes time has brought about in the city, education, and work habits. Among the other topics in this work are nature, fishing, gardening, success, and spirits--both of the departed and of the variety Prohibition prohibited.Each chapter of this book is a standalone story and if you love a good laugh, these stories are for you. In me, Leacock’s wit produced the full range of laughter: smiles, chuckles, guffaws, and some uncontrollable giggles. Also, occasionally, I found myself shedding a tear or two. (Review by Debra Lynn)

title: Moonbeams from the Larger Lunacy
description: Humorous, ironic, and sometimes cynical observations of life in 1915 from Canadian humourist Stephen Leacock. (Summary by TriciaG)

title: Hohenzollerns in America With the Bolsheviks in Berlin and Other Impossibilities
description: More stories by Canadian Stephen Leacock. Some of these stories carry over characters introduced in Further Foolishness. Some stories are humourous; some are more thoughtful. It helps to be familiar with WWI-era European politics to catch much of the humour. (Summary by TriciaG)

title: Day Before Yesterday
description: This is a volume of short stories and essays by Richard Middleton. The British poet and author wrote a large number of short pieces during his life, which are today still as entertaining as they were then. Published 1912, one year after Middleton's death, this volume collects some of the best specimens of his prose. - Summary by Carolin

title: Literary Lapses
description: Short sketches relating the humourous side of life in 1910. "Professor Leacock has made more people laugh with the written word than any other living author. One may say he is one of the greatest jesters, the greatest humorist of the age." – A. P. Herbert (Summary by TriciaG and Wikipedia)

title: Strange Stories From a Chinese Studio (selections from Volume 1)
description: "Strange Tales from a Chinese Studio" or "Strange Tales of Liaozhai") is a collection of nearly five hundred mostly supernatural tales written by Pu Songling during the early Qing Dynasty. It was written in Classical Chinese rather than Vernacular Chinese. Pu is believed to have completed the majority of the tales sometime in 1679, though he could have added entries as late as 1707. He borrows from a folk tradition of oral storytelling to put to paper a series of captivating, colorful stories, where the boundary between reality and the odd or fantastic is blurred. The cast of characters includes vixen spirits, ghosts, scholars, court officials, Taoist exorcists and beasts. Moral purposes are often inverted between humans and the supposedly degenerate ghosts or spirits, resulting in a satirical edge to some of the stories. Ghosts and spirits are often bold and trustworthy, while humans are on the other hand weak, indecisive and easily manipulated, reflecting the author's own disillusionment with his society. (Summary from Wikipedia)

title: Outspan: Tales of South Africa
description: Six poignant short stories reminiscent of life as a transport rider in the Transvaal veld in the days of the gold rush in South Africa at the end of the 19th century. From an early age Fitzpatrick believed that life should be enjoyed to the full and his honest and often moving style of writing leaves one richer for having known him. (Summary by SallyMc)

title: Lot No. 249
description: Abercrombie Smith, Edward Bellingham and William Monkhouse Lee are three students at Oxford University, sharing adjacent lodgings. When people against whom Bellingham holds a grudge are attacked, Smith starts to investigate. Is Bellingham innocent? But what are the strange noises coming from his room when he is not home? This short gothic horror story first published in 1892 is a bit outside the usual haunts of Conan Doyle and has been compared to the writings of Edgar Allan Poe and H. Rider Haggard. - Summary by Availle

title: Four Noncanonical Sherlock Holmes Short Stories
description: Although the Sherlock Holmes canon traditionally consists of four novels and 56 short stories written by Arthur Conan Doyle, there are many Sherlock Holmes stories outside the canon. Most of these noncanonical stories were written by authors other than Doyle, but there are four short stories about Holmes written by Doyle that are nonetheless excluded from the canon, for various reasons. This LibriVox album consists of these four noncanonical stories. The first story, "The Field Bazaar", was first published in 1896 in a special issue of a University of Edinburgh student newspaper called The Student. Doyle wrote this very brief story to support a fundraising event at the university, his alma mater, but most scholars consider the story to be a parody and therefore not part of the canon. The second and third stories, "The Lost Special" and "The Man with the Watches", were both published in The Strand Magazine in 1898 and both feature mysteries involving trains. These two stories are not part of the canon because neither story mentions Holmes by name, although literary scholars have proposed that the unnamed "amateur reasoner" in "The Lost Special" and the unnamed "well-known criminal investigator" in "The Man with the Watches" are intended to be Holmes, and this theory is accepted for the purposes of this LibriVox album. Doyle wrote the fourth story, "How Watson Learned the Trick", for a miniature book that was placed in Queen Mary's Dolls' House, a dollhouse built for Queen Mary in the 1920s that housed a tiny library featuring works by several famous authors of the day, the contents of which were published in 1924 for public consumption. Considered a companion piece to "The Field Bazaar" due to both stories consisting entirely of conversations between Holmes and Watson over breakfast, "How Watson Learned the Trick" is similarly excluded from the canon on the grounds of being a parody. - Summary by David Purdy Cast List: Sherlock Holmes: Peter YearsleyDr. Watson: David PurdyHerbert de Lernac: Algy PugJames: NoelVoxJames Bland: Lewis WestInspector Collins: Alan MapstoneJohn Palmer: Brant BurgissPotter Hood; Dude: Zames CurranJames McPherson; John: chuckconvrEdward; Kenyon Junction Station Master: Tomas PeterSparrow MacCoy; Earlstown Station Master: Adrian StephensCoroner; Newton Station Master: Lauren-Emma BlakeBarton Moss Station Master: MarvinManchester Station Master: Larry WilsonCollins Green Station Master: TwinkleSt. Helens Station Master: Rob MarlandNarrator: Michele Eaton Edited by: Peter Yearsley Proof listener: Dulcamara

title: Memoirs of Sherlock Holmes (version 2)
description: Sherlock Holmes, a fictional character of the late 19th and early 20th century created by Scottish author and physician Sir Arthur Conan Doyle, is a brilliant London-based "consulting detective" famous for his intellectual prowess and renowned for his enormous scope of observation, his astute logical reasoning and forensic science skills in solving difficult crimes. The Memoirs of Sherlock Holmes is a collection of Sherlock Holmes mysteries, including The Final Problem in which Holmes confronts his arch-nemesis Professor Moriarty, originally published in 1894, which are preceded by The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes and followed by The Hound of the Baskervilles. (Adapted from Wikipedia)

title: Tales of Terror and Mystery
description: Though Sir Arthur Conan Doyle is best known for his detective stories, he also wrote other short stories which are masterpieces of mystery and suspense. In some of the stories in "Tales of Terror and Mystery", a suppressed uneasiness gradually builds up and evolves into sheer terror. In others, the story line unexpectedly changes and comes to a horrific conclusion.Sit back in the comfort of your armchair and let yourself be transported to the strange but compelling world created by Sir Arthur Conan Doyle.

title: Adventures of Sherlock Holmes (version 5)
description: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes is a collection of twelve short stories by Sir Arthur Conan Doyle, featuring his fictional detective Sherlock Holmes. Each of the stories are told in a first-person style from the point of view of Dr. Watson, Holmes' associate and sidekick. The stories detail Holmes and Watson's various adventures in solving cases that befuddle others within law enforcement. This book is a collection of twelve of Doyle's favorite Sherlock Holmes stories. - Summary by TheBookBro

title: Winesburg, Ohio
description: Published in 1919, and listed on the Modern Library roster of the 20th century's 100 greatest novels in English, Winesburg, Ohio presents a series of loosely related character studies of the inhabitants of a fictional Midwestern town that together form a novel of unusual unity and vision. The inarticulate and lonely citizens of Winesburg, each with his or her own secret tale to tell, frequently relate those tales to, or through their interactions with, the character of George Willard, a young Winesburg citizen on the cusp of manhood with dreams of becoming a writer. Thus Winesburg tells the story of the townspeople's loneliness and alienation in parallel with the tale of George's own coming of age. The citizens of Winesburg are described obliquely as "grotesques," but as the introductory chapter makes clear, "The grotesques were not all horrible. Some were amusing, some almost beautiful . . ." (Description by Stewart Wills)

title: King in Yellow (part 2)
description: Robert W. Chambers (1865-1933) studied art in Paris in the late 80’s and early 90’s. Shortly after returning to America, he became popular as the writer of a number of romantic novels; he is now best known as the author of “The King In Yellow”, which is a collection of short stories with the reputation of being eerie and other-worldly. However, the last few stories in the collection, which are presented here, are essentially straightforward love stories, strongly coloured by the author’s life as an artist in France. (summary by Peter Why)

title: Toys of Peace
description: This is the fifth collection of short stories by Saki (H.H. Munro), and was published posthumously in 1923. Even so, many of the stories are quite up to the standard of those collected earlier. (Summary by Graham Redman)

title: Lieutenant and Others
description: This is a volume of short stories by Herman Cyril McNeile, better known by his pseudonym "Sapper", who is well-known until today for his haunting short stories and novels, set at the front in World War I, and based on his first-hand experiences. This collection is early, it was published in 1916, the bloodiest and most gruesome year of what would later be known as the Great War. - Summary by Carolin

title: Actions And Reactions
description: A collection of short stories by the author of the Jungle book, Kim and Just So Stories. Each story is followed by a poem, so if you like If, this may also be a book for you. - Summary by Stav Nisser.

title: Beasts and Super-Beasts
description: Saki (December 18, 1870 – November 14, 1916) was the pen name of British author Hector Hugh Munro. Saki's world contrasts the effete conventions and hypocrisies of Edwardian England with the ruthless but straightforward life-and-death struggles of nature. Nature generally wins in the end. (Summary from Wikipedia)

title: Dinner Club
description: Herman Cyril McNeile, better known as Sapper, was one of England’s most popular fiction writers during the period between World Wars I and II. He was a soldier, and his early writings mostly concerned war and the way war influenced the lives of his main characters. Because British officers were prohibited from publishing under their own names, he used the pseudonym Sapper. His best known works are ten thrillers featuring Bulldog Drummond. Sapper also wrote a great many other novels and short stories. The Dinner Club begins with each of six men, the members of the club, telling a gripping story that relates specifically to his particular line of work (the first six chapters). All but one of the chapters that follow feature different characters. Yet to greater and lesser degrees they develop themes introduced in the first half of the book by the members of The Dinner Club. (There's also not a little romance ...) - Summary by Kirsten Wever

title: Gray Mills of Farley
description: As contemporary today as it was over a century ago, this relatively unsentimental tale of labor relations still packs a punch. (Summary by BellonaTimes)

title: Brushwood Boy
description: The experiences in public school, Sandhurst and military life in India of Major George Cotter together with his adventures in the dream world he discovers and frequents. (Summary by Liam Neely)

title: Square Egg and Other Sketches
description: Eight amusing short pieces by Hector Hugh Munro (pen name Saki) who died in the First World War, collected and published posthumously in 1924. The eponymous first story although humorous includes some indications of the horror of trench warfare. Though Munro was over the age of conscription he volunteered to fight. Rothay Reynolds in an appreciation of Munro written for an earlier posthumous collection quoted the officer in command of the 22nd Royal Fusiliers (Saki’s regiment): “Poor Saki! What an admiration we all had for him, I always quoted him as one of the heroes of the war. I saw daily the appalling discomforts he so cheerfully endured. He flatly refused to take a commission or in any way to allow me to try to make him more comfortable. General Vaughan told him that a brain like his was wasted as a private soldier. He just smiled. He was absolutely splendid. What courage! The men simply loved him.” - Summary by Tim Bulkeley This recording was originally published on Legamus.eu and was later released on Librivox when US copyright permitted.

title: Reginald
description: Saki (December 18, 1870 - November 14, 1916) was the pen name of the British author Hector Hugh Munro. His witty, biting and occasionally odd short stories satirised Edwardian culture. Saki is considered a master of the short story and has been compared to O. Henry and Dorothy Parker as well as Noel Coward and Oscar Wilde (who clearly influenced Saki.) His first collection of short stories, Reginald, was published by Methuen Press in 1904 though these stories first appeared in the 'Westminster Gazette'. The stories in this collection are a foil for allowing the jaded and insider/outsider figure of Reginald to comment on some ridiculous or provincial attitude prevalent in upper class Edwardian society, although one can easily recognize these same attitudes in our society today. Long popular and well known, Saki's brilliant humour is as enjoyable now as it was almost a century ago. (Summary by Jason Xanthopoulos).

title: Chronicles of Clovis
description: This is the third collection of short stories by Saki, following on from “Reginald” and “Reginald in Russia”. Although some of the stories have characters that do not appear elsewhere in the collection, many of them are loosely centred round the young Clovis Sangrail (effectively a reincarnation of Reginald). (Summary by Graham Redman)

title: Schopenhauer in the Air
description: Sadakichi Hartmann was an art and literary critic who wrote plays, short stories and several volumes of poetry. Hauntingly beautiful, these seven early short stories dance with themes of beauty and death, love and the soul, and existence itself. As found in the book's biographical note: sometimes a great author tears the mask from life, so we may look at the truth of beauty. Summary by Nemo.

title: Stalky & Co. (More Stories)
description: This small collection puts together stories by Kipling that feature the characters from Stalky & Co. but which for some reason were not included in the book Stalky & Co. Of these the more interesting is the first, "Stalky", which introduces the characters and is full of the humour and understanding of adolescent males and their timeless jostling with adult powers that characterise the stories in Stalky & Co. "Stalky" was the first of the tales to be written, and was published in 1898, so it is difficult to understand how it came to be omitted from the book in 1899. "Regulus", while also full of humour, is a more difficult read in an age when a "proper" classical education is no longer something widely experienced. There are also other stories that cannot be included here because they are still in copyright: - "The United Idolators" and "The Propagation of Knowledge" in Debits and Credits (1926) - "The Satisfaction of a Gentleman" in The Complete Stalky & Co (1929). There is also another, "Scylla and Charybdis", unpublished in Kipling's day and only made available recently, that although not copyright in the USA, cannot be included because we do not have access to the manuscript, only an edited edition (which itself is subject to copyright). If you enjoyed Stalky & Co you will probably enjoy these additional stories.(Introduction by Tim Bulkeley)

title: Man Who Would Be King
description: The Man Who Would Be King tells the story of two British adventurers in British India who become kings of Kafiristan, a remote part of Afghanistan. It was inspired by the exploits of James Brooke, an Englishman who became the "white Raja" of Sarawak in Borneo, and by the travels of American adventurer Josiah Harlan, who claimed the title Prince of Ghor. The story was first published in The Phantom Rickshaw and other Tales (Volume Five of the Indian Railway Library, published by A H Wheeler & Co of Allahabad in 1888). It also appeared in Wee Willie Winkie and Other Stories in 1895, and in numerous later editions of that collection. It is the basis for John Huston’s 1975 film of the same name, starring Sean Connery and Michael Caine as the "kings", and Christopher Plummer as Kipling. (Interim summary by Wikipedia adapted by Philippa)

title: Just So Stories
description: The Just So Stories for Little Children, first published in 1902, were written by British author Rudyard Kipling. They are a collection of fantastic stories, typically about how various animals came to be the way they are today. (Summary by Reynard with help from Wikipedia)

title: New Arabian Nights
description: New Arabian Nights is a collection of short stories which include Robert Louis Stevenson's earliest fiction as well as those considered his best work in the genre. The first and longest story stars Prince Florizel of Bohemia who appears in the later collection of stories "More New Arabian Nights: The Dynamiter." (Summary by Don W. Jenkins)

title: How To Write Short Stories, with examples
description: Here are 10 humorous short stories by Ring Lardner (March 5, 1885 – September 25, 1933), an American sports columnist and short-story writer best known for his satirical writings on sports, marriage, and the theatre. His contemporaries Ernest Hemingway, Virginia Woolf, and F. Scott Fitzgerald all professed strong admiration for his writing. ( Wikipedia and Michele Fry)

title: In a Steamer Chair and Other Stories
description: Thirteen short stories by one of the most famous writers in his day. Robert Barr was a British Canadian short story writer and novelist, born in Glasgow, Scotland. In London of the 1890s Barr became a more prolific author - publishing a book a year - and was familiar with many of the best selling authors of his day, including Bret Harte and Stephen Crane. Most of his literary output was of the crime genre, then quite in vogue. When Arthur Conan Doyle's Sherlock Holmes stories were becoming well known, Barr published in the Idler the first Holmes parody, "The Adventures of Sherlaw Kombs" (1892), a spoof that was continued a decade later in another Barr story, "The Adventure of the Second Swag" (1904) (For these two stories, see in LibriVox Barr's The Triumphs of Eugène Valmont). Despite the jibe at the growing Holmes phenomenon Barr and Doyle remained on very good terms. Doyle describes him in his memoirs Memories and Adventures as, "a volcanic Anglo - or rather Scot American, with a violent manner, a wealth of strong adjectives, and one of the kindest natures underneath it all." (Summary by Wikipedia and David Wales)

title: Wasted Day
description: This is a delightful little story about the most successful banker on Wall Street, who finds his philanthropic side when one of his former employees is arrested and needs someone to vouch for his character.. - Summary by Carolin

title: More William (version 2)
description: More William is the second William collection in the much acclaimed Just William series by Richmal Crompton. It is a sequel to the book Just William. The book was first published in 1922, (Summary by Wikipedia)

title: More William
description: The second of Crompton's series of 39 books about William Brown, our cheeky 11 year-old protagonist. A hero to some, a dastardly villain to others, this book is structured round a year in his life. Starting with William waking up on Christmas morning and ending with him going to sleep the following Christmas Eve, there are the usual round of misadventures, misunderstanding and general mayhem in between. When a boy like William wakes up under a motto that says "A Busy Day Is A Happy Day" alongside a copy of "Things A Boy Can Do", the chaos is just around the corner. Includes the very first William short story - "Rice Mould". Often dismissed as children's literature, the first few books of William stories were probably aimed more at an adult audience. They resonate with a distinctly English humour, but there are obvious echoes from 'Tom Sawyer' and 'Huckleberry Finn'.

title: Amateur
description: On the steamer on his way to London, Austin Ford meets a young woman, who is going to London to find her missing husband. Being a specialist in finding people, Mr. Ford agrees to help her in her quest. However, something appears to be not quite right about the lady and her story... (Summary by Carolin)

title: Stateside with Lee
author: Lee Coppin
description: Stateside with Lee follows one expat's journey from Brit to bona fide US citizen — and everything he's learned along the way. Each episode, Lee explores a different corner of America: the small towns, big cities, roadside diners, national parks, and local characters that make up the real country beneath the headlines. No politics, no punditry — just genuine curiosity about the people, food, traditions, and quirks that make the USA what it is. Whether he's chasing down the best barbecue in Texas, learning why Midwesterners say "you betcha," or figuring out what exactly a "block party" is, Lee brings an outsider's eye and an insider's heart to it all. Part travel show, part culture deep-dive, part love letter to his adopted home, Stateside with Lee is for anyone who's ever wondered what America looks like through fresh eyes — and anyone who's made a new country their own.

title: Beautiful Dreamer: An Audio Drama
author: ST
description: Beautiful Dreamer is an audio drama of the life and music of America’s first professional composer, Stephen Foster.

title: The Right Expert With Bob Craig
author: therightexpertwithbobcraig
description: The Right Expert with Bob Craig is a podcast dedicated to exploring the nuances of expert witness selection and its impact on complex litigation. Designed for legal professionals, the show provides valuable insights into the art of matching experts with cases, ensuring effective communication and collaboration. Host Lisa Mosbey, along with experienced guests like Bob Craig, delves into topics such as expert retention, case strategy, and the human elements of legal proceedings. The series aims to equip attorneys with the knowledge and tools needed to enhance their litigation outcomes through strategic expert engagement.

title: MedTech Pulse
description:
MedTech Pulse with James Barraclough, powered by Metric Bio.
Everyday cutting edge innovations and technological advances unlock potential medical breakthroughs to help us live longer and healthier.
In this podcast James Barraclough sits down with some of these innovators to discover the exciting breakthroughs in the MedTech market and how we as a society stand to benefit.

title: Mx JacklyNyx Nyghtmares
author: Amberousis
description: Actual play tabletop gaming with action horror themes, focused on social, environmental, and industrial degradation.

title: Hook Em' and Book Em'
author: ATX Self-Tape Studio
description:
🎙️ Hook Em' & Book Em' - An Acting podcast.
🎭 Helping Actors navigate the world of film and modern audition process so they can succeed.
🌎 Actors from all over the world pull back the curtain on the audition process and discuss what keeps them motivated to keep performing and auditioning in the modern world.
🎥Whether you're a seasoned professional or just starting your acting journey, we deliver the insights, tips, and motivation you need to book your next role. We're here to help you...HOOK EM" & BOOK EM'!
🎬 PRESENTED BY ATX SELF-TAPE
Visit www.atxselftape.com for more!

title: Billion Dollar Blunder
description: Billion Dollar Blunder is a business-history podcast about the companies that had everything and lost it. Each episode is a cinematic deep dive into a single spectacular collapse: the fraud, the hubris, the warning nobody heeded, and the exact moment it all came apart.

title: The Hour Before Dark
description: The Hour Before Dark is a true crime podcast that dramatizes real cases — cold cases, unsolved disappearances, serial killers, infamous trials, and crimes with unexpected twists. Every story feels like a movie the listener can see in their head.

title: The Void Report
description: The Void Report is a paranormal and unexplained podcast built like a case file. Each episode opens a single file: a vanished plane, a signal from space, a photograph that shouldn't exist — and lays out the evidence, the witnesses, and the competing explanations, cold and clear. Some cases close with an answer you didn't expect. Some stay open. You never know which until the end.

title: Empires and Ashes
description: Empires & Ashes is a history podcast about the moments the world turned. Each episode is a cinematic deep dive into a single pivotal event: an empire's fall, a disaster, a rebellion, a decision made in a room that changed everything after.

title: Revenue Rocks
author: Right Revenue
description: Revenue Rocks… brought to you by Right Revenue… a place where we cut through the noise of revenue management and give you practical advice you can actually use

title: The Science of Everyday Materials
description: The hidden science of the ordinary stuff around you. Short, warm, plain-language episodes on why everyday materials behave the way they do: why glass is transparent, why metal feels cold, why ice floats, why water is wet. Evergreen curiosity science for non-experts. AI-generated and disclosed.

title: Christian Business Leadership | Watchpost Partners
author: Ashley
description: Watchpost Partners | Christian Business & Leadership is a podcast for business owners, entrepreneurs, and leaders who want to honor God through their work. Hosted by Ashley Nutter, each episode combines biblical wisdom with practical leadership and business insights to help you lead with purpose, steward your time, talents, and resources faithfully, and build a business that impacts your community and glorifies Christ. Empowering Leaders. Strengthening Businesses. Impacting Communities.

title: LUCCI
author: Darren Baker
description:
LUCCI is a high-performance podcast about building wealth, discipline, and clarity through systems.
Host Darren Baker breaks down the operating principles behind execution, resilience, health, money, and relationships—without fluff.
Expect strategy you can apply immediately: mental models, personal operating systems, and real-world lessons on building a life with control and impact.
New episodes deliver actionable frameworks—and a challenge to level up your standards.

title: Learn Dutch with Alain
author: Learn Dutch with Alain
description: Learn Dutch with Alain offers Dutch lessons for beginners through advanced learners, including grammar, vocabulary, pronunciation, and exam prep. Visit https://learnanylanguages.com/dutch/private-dutch-lessons/ for lessons and companion resources.

title: MPCC Podcast: A Rural Community College
author: Mid-Plains Community College
description: Mid-Plains Community College, a two-year college in southwestern Nebraska, is more than just a place to learn, it’s a place where journeys begin. This podcast dives into the stories that make our community college unique, from students and faculty to alumni and local voices. Each episode highlights the experiences, opportunities, and connections that show why starting at a community college can be the smartest and most rewarding way to transform your future. Whether you're exploring your options or already part of the MPCC family, join us as we share what truly makes us unique.

title: The Love Jones Experience
author: Andre Romeo & Passionate P
description: The Love Jones Experience is where poetry, R&B, hip-hop, culture, and real conversations collide—hosted by a dynamic duo bringing raw perspectives, good energy, and authentic vibes to every episode. From hot topics and new music to independent artists and lifestyle talk, this is the space where creativity, connection, and culture meet.

title: Pioneers of a More Data Driven Union
description: Elder Research presents a series of interviews that spotlight government leaders who are leveraging technology and analytics to solve complex problems for the public good. The series is hosted by Christina Ho, VP of Government Analytics & Innovation at Elder Research. Christina has decades of broad experience in public finance, policy development, strategic planning, program and financial management, accounting and auditing, data analytics, and technology innovation. Elder Research is a data science consultancy with over 25 year of experience in hand-crafting innovative analytics solutions that inform decisions, deliver value, and transform organizations.

title: Everything in Life
author: Everything in Life
description: A podcast where we discussed everyone life.

title: This Old Story - Fables and Folktales
description: Family-friendly retellings of fables and short tales from world traditions, followed by thoughtful discussion about choices, values, and meaning.

title: Reformation Presbyterian Church Sermons
author: Joshua Roberts
description: Welcome to the sermon podcast of Reformation Presbyterian Church in Pike Road, Alabama. Our desire is to proclaim the whole counsel of God through faithful, Christ-centered exposition of the Scriptures. New sermons are added weekly following each Lord's Day service.

title: A Most Unpredictable Life
author: Jeremy Lewis
description: Jeremy Lewis wasn’t voted Most Talented. He wasn’t voted Best Looking. He definitely wasn’t voted Most Athletic.  He was voted Most Unpredictable — and his life has been proving it ever since.  A Most Unpredictable Life is a year‑by‑year storytelling podcast hosted by Jeremy and his good friend of eight years, Terry Lewis. Together they jump to a different year of Jeremy’s life each episode, piecing together a story that moves from a chaotic childhood — including a summer living in a van and motels — to cross‑country moves, five brothers, a $500 car headed for Chicago, Army Airborne School, deployments around the world, Tesla leadership, and even a flaming snowman operation that became campus legend.  Jeremy tells the stories. Terry opens the door, asks the questions, and keeps him honest — the kind of dynamic only two friends who genuinely enjoy each other can pull off.  One week you’re in 1992 cruising Ashland, Ohio in a ’65 Mustang with November Rain blasting. The next week you’re in 1973 learning where the story really began. Then you’re in Bosnia, Iraq, Afghanistan, or back in a Chicago dorm where military cadets accidentally start a campus legend.  It’s funny up front, real in the middle, and leaves you feeling good every time. A life that didn’t fit the usual superlatives ended up becoming one hell of a story.  If your life hasn’t gone in a straight line either, you’re in the right place.

title: Pastor Sherry Lupton Sunday Sermons
author: Shiloh Presbyterian Church
description:
Welcome to the Pastor Sherry Lupton Sermon Podcast, the weekly teaching ministry of Shiloh Presbyterian Church.

Each episode features the Sunday morning sermon from Pastor Sherry Lupton, thoughtfully condensed and presented to help you stay rooted in God's Word throughout the week. Whether you missed a service, want to revisit a message that spoke to your heart, or are looking for biblical encouragement on the go, these sermons are here to inspire, challenge, and strengthen your faith.

Join us as we explore Scripture, grow in Christ, and discover practical, hope-filled truths for everyday life.

"Faithfully proclaiming God's Word. Transforming lives through His grace."

title: Bomwin
author: bomwincapital
description: Bomwin - Các danh mục được sắp xếp khoa học giúp người dùng dễ dàng tiếp cận nội dung mong muốn chỉ với vài thao tác đơn giản. Hệ thống thường xuyên được bảo trì và nâng cấp để bổ sung các tiện ích mới, đồng thời cải thiện hiệu năng tổng thể. Điều này góp phần mang lại trải nghiệm giải trí liền mạch và thuận tiện trên nhiều thiết bị khác nhau. WEB: https://bomwin.capital/

title: BomWin
author: bomwinworks
description: BomWin - Hệ thống chú trọng xây dựng môi trường sử dụng an toàn với nhiều lớp bảo mật hiện đại nhằm bảo vệ thông tin cá nhân của người dùng. Dữ liệu được quản lý cẩn thận, hạn chế các rủi ro phát sinh trong quá trình truy cập. Ngoài ra, nền tảng còn áp dụng nhiều biện pháp kiểm tra và giám sát nhằm duy trì hiệu suất ổn định. Nhờ vậy, người dùng có thể yên tâm khám phá các nội dung giải trí và tận hưởng trải nghiệm WEB: https://bomwin.works/

title: The Overflow State
author: Heike Bitterle
description:
Remember that state where your ideas effortlessly drop in, your words flow through you, and you feel so alive and fulfilled by the work you get to do?
This creative inner state is not luck—it is your natural state. In this podcast, we are bringing your system home to it.
Hosted by Heike Bitterle, 2x solo female founder and former perfectionist sharing raw experiences & holding powerful convos to bring your inner and outer world to that state where creativity flows, visibility feels natural, and your impact grows steady and stable.
Hit subscribe. Let’s bring your system home.

title: What Comes Next with Arun
author: Arunansu Pattanayak
description: Most conversations about AI are either too technical for business leaders or too generic to be useful. What Comes Next with Arun fills that gap. Each episode translates real-world data and AI strategy into the language of competitive advantage — drawing on Arun’s 20+ years inside the world’s most complex enterprises, six years as a Microsoft Data & AI Executive, and his experience building Tipsora into a platform serving more than 95,000 professionals worldwide. This is not a podcast about AI tools. It is a podcast about building the organizational intelligence that makes tools matter.

title: Stoic Habits: Daily Mastery
description:
Unlock your inner resilience and cultivate a life of purpose. Stoic Habits: Daily Mastery delivers timeless wisdom for modern challenges.

This podcast is your daily guide to applying ancient Stoic philosophy to contemporary life. Discover practical habits for emotional regulation, rational decision-making, and finding tranquility amidst chaos. We distill the profound teachings of Seneca, Marcus Aurelius, and Epictetus into actionable strategies you can implement immediately.

New episodes arrive daily, Monday through Sunday, promptly at 8:00 AM. Each short, impactful episode focuses on a specific Stoic concept or exercise, designed to seamlessly integrate into your morning routine and set a powerful tone for your day.

Stoic Habits: Daily Mastery is for anyone seeking personal growth, mental fortitude, and a more fulfilling existence. If you're ready to transform your approach to stress, adversity, and everyday choices, this is your essential daily practice.

Subscribe now and start mastering your day, every day.

title: Still HER Beyond the Titles
author: Jasmine Quinnette
description: Still HER: Beyond the Titles is a podcast for the woman behind the roles. Hosted by Jasmine Quinnette, this space explores womanhood, identity, motherhood, marriage, growth, and rebuilding yourself beyond the expectations you carry. Through honest conversations and reflections, Still HER helps women reconnect with who they are beneath the titles. This isn’t about becoming someone new—it’s about rediscovering yourself, gently and honestly, one step at a time. Because beyond every role and responsibility… she’s still HER.

title: Niños Anclados En Cristo
author: Stay Anchored Ministries
description: ¡Bienvenidos a **Niños Anclados en Cristo**!  Un podcast diseñado para ayudar a los niños y a sus familias a conocer toda la Biblia, fortalecer su fe y aprender a confiar en Dios en cada etapa de la vida.  En cada episodio recorreremos la Biblia en orden cronológico, desde Génesis hasta Apocalipsis, descubriendo las grandes historias, los personajes bíblicos y, sobre todo, el maravilloso plan de Dios para la humanidad.  También responderemos preguntas importantes de una manera sencilla y apropiada para niños, ayudándolos a entender no solo **qué** creen, sino también **por qué** lo creen.  Este podcast es perfecto para: • Devocionales en familia • Viajes en el automóvil • Educación en el hogar (homeschool) • Ministerios infantiles • Niños que desean crecer en su relación con Jesús  Nuestro deseo es levantar una nueva generación de niños firmemente anclados en la verdad de la Palabra de Dios, preparados para vivir su fe con amor, confianza y valentía.  ¡Acompáñanos en esta gran aventura por toda la Biblia!  **Niños Anclados en Cristo** es un ministerio de **Stay Anchored Ministries**.

title: Lexpo Talks
author: Hidde Bruinsma
description:
Lexpo Talks examines the ways technology is reshaping the legal profession. Host Ruben Havertz sits down with founders, general counsels and innovators who are redefining how lawyers think, work and lead in an era driven by AI.


The emphasis is on genuine experiences rather than buzz. Guests open up about what succeeds, what falls short and how legal teams can hold on to their humanity while preparing for what's next.

If you're curious about where law and technology actually intersect, this is where that conversation begins.


Want me to try a few alternative tones as well for example a punchier, shorter version or a more formal one?

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: REAL CHRISTIANITY TODAY
author: Emmanuel & Chizoba
description: Real Christianity Today Podcast is a place we have authentic, honest, Scripture-centered conversations about what it really means to follow Christ in today's world. This is for modern Christians who want to navigate today's culture while staying rooted in biblical truth.

title: Anchored with Heather Moon Vogels
author: Heather Moon Vogels
description:
Conversations about identity, transition, finding steadiness through change and what it means to be whole - with psychologist, trauma therapist and rower Heather Moon Vogels.
Anchored is for anyone navigating a major life transition — whether that’s life after elite sport, becoming a mother, moving countries, or simply asking who you are when your context changes completely. Psychologist, trauma therapist, and former competitive rower Heather Moonvogels brings together the worlds of high performance and trauma-informed healing — exploring identity, nervous system regulation.

title: JuJuBabyWorld
author: JuJuBaby
description: Bay Area Music Artist talking about life and music.

title: The Ashley Hepperly Podcast
author: Ashley Hepperly & Rooted Collective
description:
Hey, I’m Ashley!


I’m just out here trying to help others walk deeply with Jesus and have adventure with God by knowing his wild love. 


And I have a ministry called Rooted Collective where we do just that. 


There's a way not many choose--a hidden way--where the deepest healing happens beneath the surface, in the roots of the heart.

This spot is for heart healing, authentic community, and raw conversations about walking with God in intimacy, identity, and wholeness…and well, some storytelling and fun. 

Do you long to dig into the deep parts of God? To be fully alive in Jesus from the inside out, truly living in radical love? If so, this is your invitation.


Hang out with me here virtually in the Great Smoky Mountains of East Tennessee. I'll sometimes bring friends on to talk about healing the heart and experiencing God together. See you on the inside:)

title: Ambient Alleys
author: Katrin Chisholm
description: Unwind with our latest sleep podcast and tranquil tales designed to help you drift into serenity. Explore stories that ease your mind and nurture quiet moments.

title: GNN Daily Breaking News
author: Castro
description:
This podcast delivers sharp political commentary, cultural analysis, and headline breakdowns, with a tone that resonates with viewers of Fox News, Newsmax, and other conservative-leaning outlets. Whether you're tuning in for takes on breaking news, media bias, or global affairs, CNN News Daily offers clarity, conviction, and unapologetic truth-telling.

NOT AFFILIATED TO ANY.

title: Reel for Real - Behind the Scenes
author: Sonia W Kohli
description:
Real stories beyond the reel.
Reel for Real – Behind the Scenes is a podcast exploring identity, perception and the realities behind what we see and perceive.
Created and hosted by Sonia W Kohli, the series goes beyond surface-level conversations to explore the person behind the image, the confidence, the success, the smile and the stories we don’t always see.
Produced by Kunishka Productions.

title: Inspiring Entrepreneurs
author: Podplistic
description:
The Inspiring Entrepreneurs Podcast brings you insightful conversations with entrepreneurs and thought leaders from diverse industries in Scotland and beyond. Join us as we dive into the challenges, successes, and hot topics currently shaping the world of entrepreneurship. Whether you’re a curious citizen, an experienced practitioner, or a political actor, we’ll provide you with real-world inspiration to fuel your journey.

 

Brought to you by Strathclyde Inspire at the University of Strathclyde, Scotland – supporting entrepreneurship in all its forms. Find out more: www.strath.ac.uk/workwithus/strathclydeinspire/

title: Eurth Deep Dive
author: Eurth Worldbuilding and Roleplay Writing Community
description:
Eurth Deep Dive is a podcast that dives into the collaborative roleplaying conworld called of Eurth.
Join our hosts, Momerator and Dadmin, as they explore its nations and cultures, analyse major events from geopolitics and history, and uncover the stories behind our ever-evolving worldbuilding project.
Whether you're an Eurth player or a curious worldbuilding enthusiast, this Eurth Deep Dive offers fascinating insights into our collaborative storytelling and fictional world creation.
For more information, visit https://eurth.org
— Eurth Worldbuilding and Roleplay Writing Community

title: Life Without A Clue
author: KeKe and Nikki
description: A witty, hilarious, serious, emotional, and informative podcast shedding light on Millennials navigating through life without a clue. There’s no blueprint or handbooks on this journey, just life lessons and experiences molding us into who we’re destined to be.

title: CHAMBER MODE PODCAST
author: Four Chambers Media
description: Come Listen To Unheard of Stories, Messy Stories, Weird and Cringe stories right Here!!!!

title: The Guess What Podcast
author: Troy and Larissa
description:
The Guess What Podcast brings you hilarious story times, real conversations, and the “wait, WHAT?” moments of adult life, all told from two sides of the same situation.
It’s relatable, chaotic, honest, and the perfect mix of comedy and connection!

title: Outrigger Tour
author: Outrigger Outdoors
description: Outrigger Tour takes you behind the scenes with guides, captains, hunters, anglers, and outdoor industry leaders who live the outdoor lifestyle. From bowfishing, flounder gigging, and bully netting to duck hunting, predator hunting, airboats, gear, and conservation, each episode features real conversations, expert tips, and authentic stories from the people who know it best. Real people. Real stories. Real outdoors.

title: The Gear Up Podcast
author: Erin White
description:
After 15+ years of coaching fastpitch softball catchers, one thing became clear: no catcher’s journey should be ever built on guesswork. Yet every day, too many catchers, parents, and coaches are left to navigate the hardest position on the field without a clear, proven path forward.
The Gear Up Podcast was created to change that.
Every week, we help catchers, parents, and coaches stop guessing, stop crossing their fingers, and start developing with purpose.
Hosted by Coach Erin White, founder of Gear Up Catching, each episode is designed to help you better understand what really goes into developing great catchers through practical coaching, proven strategies, and honest conversations.
Whether you’re learning the position for the first time, supporting your daughter’s softball journey, or coaching the next generation of catchers, you’ll gain practical tools you can put into action immediately.
Inside the show, we’ll cover everything from catching training, skill development, game management, and softball IQ to leadership, mindset, communication, recruiting, total athlete development, and what it truly takes to become the complete catcher.
Drawing from more than 15 years of coaching experience, a Master’s Degree in Education and Curriculum Design, and a track record of helping more than 1,500 catchers develop their game, Coach Erin shares the same philosophies and proven systems she has used throughout her coaching career. Those same principles have helped 100% of graduating Gear Up athletes who pursued college softball go on to successfully achive their dreams of playing at the next level, while earning nearly $7 million in scholarship opportunities.
This podcast goes beyond tips for building elite catchers.
It’s about developing the entire catcher, and the human behind the gear.
One who is confident behind the plate, leads with courage, competes with purpose, and carries those lessons far beyond the game.
Our mission is simple: to equip catchers, parents, and coaches with the knowledge, tools, and confidence to develop confident catchers, fearless leaders behind the plate, and warriors for life.

title: IT Corner
description:
IT Corner Podcast, is our YouTube channel for the IT podcasts
What can you expect from our podcast?
- Monthly podcast with new guests from the IT industry 
- Inspiration topics and motivation thoughts
- TOP trends in the IT technology
- How to start your IT career 
Separate series related to different topics and categories
- Playlist with a full podcast

title: Playing the Long Game
author: Yusuf Bodiat
description: Playing the Long Game. Yusuf Bodiat — CFO, author of The Bottom Line, and founder of Pragmatic Thinking — on how serious decisions get made, why most strategy fails, and what long-term thinking actually looks like in business, leadership, and finance. Season 1 launching soon.

title: Scaling Trust with Matt Tidwell
author: Matt Tidwell
description: Scaling Trust is a show about what it actually takes to build durable relationships between companies and their customers — beyond the handoff, beyond onboarding, and into the kind of trust that drives expansion and advocacy.

title: Mic'd and Messy
author: Travis Conway and Jonnie Hubbard
description: Mic’d and Messy is a podcast that dives into honest, unfiltered conversations about navigating the nonprofit world. Each episode explores the real challenges behind mission-driven work—fundraising, burnout, leadership, limited resources, and impact while also highlighting the messy, unpredictable, and human side of making a difference. It’s a space for candid insights, lessons learned, and practical strategies from people working on the front lines of change.

title: THE WORLD'S BEST PREACHER
author: JAKES T_D
description: Welcome to "the world's best preacher" This is a space for healing, restoration, and stepping into the purpose God placed in you. Like Bishop T.D. Jakes says, it’s time to move past your pain and into your power. We talk faith, real life, relationships, and that inner instinct God gave you to win. You’re not broken. You’re being built. Tune in, and don’t drop the mic on your destiny.

title: MACWEALTH AUDIOBOOKS
author: Isaiah Macwealth
description:
Macwealth Audiobooks is the official audiobook podcast of Prophet Isaiah Macwealth, featuring powerful Christian audiobooks, biblical teachings, devotionals, and faith-building messages designed to deepen your relationship with God.


Listen to bestselling Christian books, including The Morning Altar Warrior, along with teachings on prayer, spiritual warfare, the Holy Spirit, revival, healing, kingdom living, leadership, and Christian discipleship.


Whether you're praying, commuting, or studying God's Word, each episode is created to inspire spiritual growth, strengthen your faith, and equip you to live a victorious Christian life.


Subscribe today and experience the transforming power of God's Word—one chapter at a time.

title: HAVANA SYNDROME IS HERE
author: HSIH
description:
Thank you for listening. This podcast is not perfect. I am a victim trying to speak up.
On this podcast, every idea, every question, and every logical inference is crucial.
Havana Syndrome is here in the United States, and it’s happening to ordinary people too - like myself, for 5 years now.
Havana Syndrome is one of the biggest Civil and Constitutional rights issue in a long time.
Most people are not thinking like that, because they are still thinking a foreign government is responsible.
Is Havana Syndrome, a Constitutional Crisis in the United States?

title: The Avengers – Old Time Radio Spy (OTR)
description:
🎧 Welcome to The Avengers, a classic Old Time Radio spy drama that brings you a delightful blend of humor and intrigue from the 1940s. This series features a variety of comedic characters navigating through varied scenarios, all while delivering sharp wit and engaging narratives.

📻 Each episode showcases the clever interplay of dialogue and situational comedy, making it a unique entry in the Old Time Radio landscape. Expect to encounter a range of espionage-themed sketches that highlight the absurdities of spy life, complete with lighthearted banter and memorable moments.

🎭 The Avengers remains a notable series among collectors for its charming performances by classic radio actors and its ability to entertain through clever storytelling. Fans of vintage radio will appreciate the comedic elements that set this show apart from more serious spy dramas.

🔎 Tune in for full-length broadcasts of The Avengers, where classic OTR spy adventures await, filled with humor and nostalgia.

title: Architects of AI
author: Metric Search
description: Welcome Architects of AI with Zeke Arlott. Each week Zeke will be joined by a pioneer in AI - founders, leaders and investors representing some of the most exciting and technologically advanced AI companies in the world.

title: Theatre Royal – Old Time Radio Drama (OTR)
description:
🎧 Welcome to Theatre Royal, a distinguished Old Time Radio drama series that showcases the finest theatrical performances from the golden age of radio. This series presents a rich tapestry of dramatic narratives, featuring adaptations of classic literature and original plays that resonate with the essence of live theatre.

📻 Each episode transports listeners to various theatrical settings, where a diverse array of characters come to life through the talents of classic radio performers and actors. Expect engaging storytelling that captures the spirit of the stage, with themes ranging from romance to tragedy, all delivered in a format that mirrors the excitement of live performances.

🎭 Theatre Royal remains a notable series among collectors and enthusiasts of vintage broadcasts, appealing to those who appreciate the art of drama and the craft of storytelling. With its high-quality productions, this series is a must-listen for fans of theatrical adaptations and classic radio drama.

🔎 Join us for full-length episodes of Theatre Royal, where the magic of Old Time Radio drama unfolds in every broadcast, preserving the legacy of theatrical storytelling for generations to come.

title: FanBuzz Weekly
author: FanBuzz Weekly
description: Hosted by FanBuzz writers Tyler Brooke, Patrick Pinak and Will Schwartz, we dive into the biggest news and hottest takes in sports. Tune in for your weekly dose of athletic insanity. Trust me, this podcast is a winner.

title: Protect Florida
author: Sierra Club Florida
description:
Protect Florida is a Sierra Club Florida storytelling project sharing powerful stories from the people and communities fighting for Florida’s environment, wildlife, climate, and future. Through in-depth conversations and frontline reporting, each episode explores what is at stake - and how Floridians can take action.
Learn more and take action: sierraclub.org/protectflorida

title: In the Making
author: Kezia Tan
description: this is a space where real stories reveal God’s shaping work, leading listeners toward reflection, conviction, encouragement, and a deeper commitment to live out their faith.

title: Nittany Talk After Hours
author: Nikolai Wagner and Shane Rayzis
description: Nikolai Wagner and Shane Rayzis host a comedic after-show podcast about Nittany Talk, a late-night comedy show from Penn State Network Television. Each week, they will have a guest from Nittany Talk, and during the episode, they will interview the guest, talk about the show that happened that week, and hopefully be funny.

title: Leadership SOPs Systems
author: Ed Tyson
description:
Leadership SOPs is a podcast for leaders who want to move beyond managing teams and learn how to create alignment, influence, and results across entire organizations.

Hosted by leadership author, coach, and consultant Ed Tyson, the show explores the real work of leadership, especially in complex environments where success depends on collaboration across teams, departments, and stakeholders. Each episode breaks down practical leadership challenges and provides frameworks, tools, and insights to help leaders build stronger communities of effort, improve organizational effectiveness, and lead with greater clarity and consistency.

Whether you're an executive, manager, or emerging leader, Leadership SOPs helps you navigate the complexities of modern leadership and develop the skills needed to drive meaningful results through

title: Between Worlds - Regenerative Wayfinding Architecture for Urban Leaders
author: Menno Lammers
description: We are living between worlds: extraction and regeneration, past and future urban systems, and old and emerging leadership. Welcome to Between Worlds, the Regenerative Wayfinding Architecture Podcast for urban leaders in transition, hosted by Menno Lammers. Each episode features pioneers and changemakers reshaping leadership, cities, and business as a force for life. For those navigating complexity and seeking direction in systemic change, this is a space to wayfind together.

title: The True North Pod
author: The True North Pod
description:
The thing holding Canada back isn't Trump, Trudeau or tariffs. It's us.
We have everything we need to win, we just don't know it yet. 
The True North Pod is here change that.
Hosted by Gregory Foran and backed by The True North Fund, we tell the raw stories of the Canadian founders who built billion-dollar businesses. 
Raw. Unfiltered. Real.
Not polished PR interviews. We uncover the failures, near-death moments, and obsession required to win—from world-class self-made CEOs like Jeff Shiner (1Password) to Olympic legends.
It's our time. 
Let's build. 🇨🇦

title: All About Autonomy
author: Metric
description:
All About Autonomy with Zeke Arlott, powered by Metric. 

Autonomous vehicles are one of the biggest technical advances in recent history with the market now estimated to be worth over $40 billion with predictions of further growth over the coming years.

In this podcast Zeke Arlott sits down with some of the industries biggest names, as well as market disrupters who are providing ground breaking solutions in this fascinating industry.

title: Divinely Unfiltered
author: divinelyunfilteredpodcast
description:
Welcome to Divinely Unfiltered — not your mama's Bible study!

Four women. Real faith. Real talk. Real life.

This is the podcast for women who are done with surface-level Christianity and ready to go deeper. We dig into Scripture the way it was meant to be studied — in context, in the original languages, and applied to real everyday life. What started as four years of friendship, prayer, and serious Bible study has turned into Divinely Unfiltered. So grab a cup of coffee, a diet coke, or a sweet tea, and LET'S GO!

title: Dear Dark Diary
author: Virelle Studios
description: For the words you buried, the love you couldn’t hold onto, the truths that haunt you when the world goes quiet. Through anonymous confessions and raw, unfiltered emotion, this podcast lingers in the spaces you try to ignore. It gives a voice to the emotions we bury and the truths we avoid. No filters. No judgment. Just honesty. If you’ve ever felt too much, said too little, or struggled to explain what’s inside you, this is your safe space.

title: Max88
description: Max88 - Dữ liệu được quản lý cẩn thận, hạn chế các rủi ro phát sinh trong quá trình truy cập. Ngoài ra, nền tảng còn áp dụng nhiều biện pháp kiểm tra và giám sát nhằm duy trì hiệu suất ổn định. Nhờ vậy, người dùng có thể yên tâm khám phá các nội dung giải trí và tận hưởng trải nghiệm thuận tiện trong suốt thời gian sử dụng mà không gặp nhiều phiền toái. WEB: https://max88m.com/

title: The Goldbergs – Old Time Radio Soap Opera
description:
🎧 Welcome to The Goldbergs, a classic Old Time Radio soap opera that captures the essence of family life in the 1930s and 1940s. This beloved series, created by Gertrude Berg, revolves around the everyday experiences of the Goldberg family, showcasing their joys, struggles, and humorous interactions.

📻 The Goldbergs is characterized by its rich storytelling and relatable characters, including the matriarch Molly Goldberg, who navigates the challenges of family life with warmth and wit. Listeners can expect a delightful blend of comedy and drama, as the series explores themes of love, tradition, and the immigrant experience in America.

🎭 As a staple of vintage radio, The Goldbergs remains a cherished part of the OTR landscape, appealing to collectors and fans of family dramas and radio serials. Its unique portrayal of Jewish family life and cultural nuances makes it a standout in the genre.

🔎 Enjoy full-length broadcasts of this classic radio soap opera, perfect for fans of vintage radio and nostalgic storytelling.

title: Rooted Christianity
author: Sarah Thompson
description: Various trials come at us as Christians, I want to help you have a deeply rooted life in God so you can weather every storm and come out stronger in the end.

title: That's CLASSIFIED with Michael Schratt
author: The UneXplained Network
description:
THAT’S CLASSIFIED(with Michael Schratt)

That’s Classified, Michael Schratt (military aerospace historian) brings the audience through a magical mystery tour of defense contractor “Black Projects”. With over 35 years of experience within the mechanical engineering/aerospace industry, Michael brings a unique “nuts-and-bolts” perspective to the topic. This weekly program explores subjects such as: UFO Crash/Retrievals, Classified aircraft, secret government underground facilities, historical UFO cases, Unidentified Submerged Objects, early stealth legacy, The Hudson Valley Boomerang, Cash/Landrum incident, Black Triangles, Air force Plant 42 and the “Aerospace alley”, Two-stage-to-orbit Spaceplane, the Reagan military build-up, fraud, waste, and abuse in government spending and many more.

     Through actual “boots-on-the-ground” investigations, this program aims to provide a “paper-trail” so that the audience can verify the information that is being presented. Every effort has been made to approach this subject from an academic perspective while also providing newspaper articles, technical papers and documents as supporting evidence. The primary goal of this program is to alert both the American taxpayer and concerned citizens around the world to a very deep/clandestine level of technology that has been kept “under wraps” for over 90 years. Through the use of sketches, drawings, illustrations, and full color renderings associated with TOP SECRET aerospace programs, this program endeavors to preserve an important part of our national history.

title: BRAMPTON NEWS
author: Brampton Today
description: News, weather, events and more in the City of Brampton, Ontario

title: The Modern Homes Podcast
description: Join us as we explore the world of options for your ideal house in an in depth, but also a fun way. Learn all about Real Estate, Home Building and Remodeling with Rob, Frank and the occasional guest expert speaker!

title: B52Club
